21.correct: Birsa Munda, the Jharkhand tribal nationalist known as 'Dharti Baba', led the Munda rebellion (Ulgulan) against British exploitation and is commemorated in the Indian Parliament Museum.

wrong.B: Alluri Sitarama Raju led a revolt in Andhra Pradesh.
wrong.C: Komaram Bheem was a Gond leader from Telangana.
wrong.D: Pir Ali Khan was a 1857 rebel from Bihar.

21.fact: Birsa Munda's birth anniversary (15 November) is celebrated as Janjatiya Gaurav Divas.

22.correct: In September 2024, Alok Ranjan was appointed the head (Director) of the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB).

wrong.A: Rajnit Kohli is not the appointee.
wrong.C: Ambuj Chandna is not the NCRB head.
wrong.D: Gyanesh Kumar is associated with the Election Commission.

22.fact: The NCRB compiles and publishes crime statistics in India.

23.correct: In May 2025, the eminent Indian nuclear scientist Dr M.R. Srinivasan passed away.

wrong.B: Dr A.P.J. Abdul Kalam died in 2015.

23.fact: Dr M.R. Srinivasan played a key role in developing India's nuclear power programme.

24.correct: The heart is NOT part of the human endocrine system (it is part of the circulatory system).

wrong.B: The hypothalamus is an endocrine structure controlling the pituitary.
wrong.C: The pancreas is an endocrine (and exocrine) gland.
wrong.D: The ovary is an endocrine gland producing female hormones.

24.fact: The endocrine system consists of glands that secrete hormones directly into the blood.

25.correct: The heartbeat is an example of an involuntary action - it happens automatically without conscious control.
25.fact: Involuntary actions are controlled mainly by the autonomic nervous system and the medulla.

26.correct: TrailGuard AI, set up at Similipal Tiger Reserve in February 2025, is a system of AI-enabled cameras for wildlife monitoring and anti-poaching alerts.

wrong.B: It is not a flood early-warning system.
wrong.C: It is not a payments app.
wrong.D: It is not an environmental-clearance app.

26.fact: AI-enabled camera networks help detect poachers and protect wildlife in real time.

27.correct: Venus is a rocky (terrestrial) planet, NOT made of gases and liquids like the gas giants.
27.fact: The four inner planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars) are rocky; the outer four are giant gas/ice planets.

28.correct: 'Khuded' folk songs are traditionally associated with Uttarakhand.
28.fact: Khuded songs express the longing/separation felt by married women in the Garhwal hills.

29.correct: 'Lemlawi', an instrument of the jaw-harp family, is a traditional instrument of Mizoram.
29.fact: Jaw harps are small plucked instruments found among many tribal communities.

30.correct: The evidence from the Indus Valley Civilization shows that its people were excellent engineers (with planned cities, drainage and the Great Bath).

wrong.A: They were settled urban dwellers, not largely nomadic.
wrong.C: They had advanced, not absent, urban-planning skills.
wrong.D: They built wells and reservoirs, not relying only on rain.

30.fact: Harappan cities had grid layouts, standardised bricks and sophisticated drainage systems.

31.correct: Both statements are correct - soil's physical properties are affected by trees/vegetation (I), and removing the topsoil exposes harder, rocky layers beneath (II).

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are correct.

31.fact: Deforestation accelerates soil erosion and loss of fertile topsoil.

32.correct: At the 2022 Commonwealth Games, South Africa finished below India in the medal tally.

wrong.C: Australia topped the table, above India.

32.fact: India finished 4th in the 2022 CWG medal table with 61 medals.

33.correct: Jammu and Kashmir won the maximum medals at the National Games 2025.

wrong.B: Andaman & Nicobar Islands did not top the medals.
wrong.C: Chandigarh did not win the most.
wrong.D: Puducherry did not top the tally.

33.fact: The National Games are India's biggest multi-sport domestic competition.

34.correct: Both viruses (I) and bacteria (II) can cause several diseases in poultry, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both cause poultry diseases.

34.fact: Diseases like bird flu (viral) and fowl cholera (bacterial) affect poultry.

35.correct: In Malay, the name 'orangutan' means 'man of the forest' ('orang' = person, 'hutan' = forest).

wrong.B: The Sumatran tiger's name does not mean this.
wrong.C: The Komodo dragon's name does not mean 'man of the forest'.
wrong.D: The Irrawaddy dolphin's name does not mean this.

35.fact: Orangutans are great apes found in the rainforests of Borneo and Sumatra.

36.correct: Apis florea is known as the 'little bee' (little honeybee).

wrong.A: Apis cerana indica is the Indian hive bee.
wrong.B: Apis dorsata is the giant/rock bee.
wrong.D: Apis mellifera is the European honeybee.

36.fact: Apis florea makes small, single-comb nests and produces little honey.

37.correct: Nitrogen is a component of both proteins (I) and chlorophyll (II) in living organisms, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since nitrogen is in both.

37.fact: Nitrogen is also a key component of nucleic acids (DNA and RNA).

38.correct: The 'India Discrimination Report' was published by Oxfam India.

wrong.B: The Centre for Equity Studies publishes other reports.
wrong.C: Tata Trusts did not publish this report.
wrong.D: Praja Foundation did not publish it.

38.fact: Oxfam India publishes reports on inequality and discrimination.

39.correct: The 19th-century philosopher and reformer Dayananda Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj (in 1875).

wrong.A: Swami Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission.
wrong.C: Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded the Brahmo Samaj.
wrong.D: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar was a reformer but did not found the Arya Samaj.

39.fact: The Arya Samaj gave the call to 'go back to the Vedas' and opposed social evils.

40.correct: The 'Global Trade Outlook and Statistics 2025' was published by the World Trade Organization (WTO).

wrong.B: The IMF did not publish it.
wrong.D: The World Bank did not publish it.

40.fact: The WTO regularly publishes forecasts and statistics on world merchandise and services trade.
